response_constraints: Ground every reported ID, date, and update outcome in the corresponding tool response.
After the correction turn, report the stable ID and corrected final date without implying that anyone was notified.
Treat 2026-12-17 as the final date and 2026-09-17 only as the superseded interim date.
Do not claim that a notification, cancellation, recreation, profile lookup, list, availability check, or unrelated-record update occurred.

stages.purpose: Resolve one stable ID and its original date from the user's name-and-location description before any mutation.
Apply the first date instruction only after the target ID is established in the prior turn.
Apply the correction to the same stable ID; the later instruction supersedes the earlier date.

follow_up_turns.content: That is the subscription I meant. Change only its date to 2026-09-17. Do not touch either nearby record and do not notify anyone.
Correction: replace my previous date with 2026-12-17 for that same subscription. This supersedes 2026-09-17; do not cancel or recreate the record, and still send no notification.

prompt: I’m reconciling the subscription filed as “Family fiber 500 plan” at Residential Accounts, but I do not know its record ID. Locate it and tell me the ID and current date from the search result. This first turn is read-only: do not open another record, edit, cancel, create, or notify anything. After the correction turn, report the stable ID and corrected final date without implying that anyone was notified.
